VAL_METRIC: 0.5541818181818181
analysis: Tested a highly complex synthetic geometric generation procedure that introduced an outer "edema" shell (dilated mask) mapped differentially across the 4 MRI modalities (FLAIR, T1, T1c, T2). While biologically plausible for Glioblastoma, this approach failed to improve on Iteration 13, achieving a max validation AUC of 0.5542. The complexity of randomly mixing structural boundaries across channels during pre-training likely confused the network, generating textures/gradients that don't actually align well with real structural boundaries in the downstream MRI data. Simple, sharp structural geometry generation paired with aggressive BraTS data augmentation (as in Iteration 13) remains the most robust strategy.
